perturbation.original_text: THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION agrees to use its best efforts to restrict the uses of PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content by visitors to its Web Pages to personal use of such Content and not for further commercial redistribution.
perturbation.changed_text: THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION will endeavor to encourage visitors to its Web Pages to primarily use PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content for personal use and discourage further commercial redistribution where feasible.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates an obligation for 'best efforts' to restrict commercial redistribution, which is relatively clear. The modified text weakens this significantly by changing 'agrees to use its best efforts' to 'will endeavor to encourage,' making the restriction discretionary. The addition of 'where feasible' further reduces the enforceability of this clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 17200 prohibits unfair competition, which includes unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent business acts or practices. By weakening the restriction on commercial redistribution, the modified clause may facilitate or allow unfair competition if the content is commercially redistributed without proper authorization or compensation to the original content creator (Pacificap), especially if it harms the market for Pacificap's content.

GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 5. Enforcement [17200 - 17210] ( Chapter 5 added by Stats. 1977, Ch. 299. ) 17200. As used in this chapter, unfair competition shall mean and include any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business act or practice and unfair, deceptive, untrue or misleading advertising and any act prohibited by Chapter 1 (commencing with Section 17500) of Part 3 of Division 7 of the Business and Professions Code. (Amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 430, Sec. 2. Effective January 1, 1993.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the original contract's obligation to restrict commercial redistribution, potentially leading to unfair competition as described in the Law Explanation. The original text commits THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION to 'best efforts to restrict' while the changed text only commits to 'endeavor to encourage' and 'discourage where feasible.' This shift weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that unfair competition includes any 'unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business act or practice.' The weakening of restrictions on content redistribution could facilitate unfair competition if Pacificap's content is commercially redistributed without authorization, contradicting BPC 17200.

perturbation.original_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT will provide on-going assistance to THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, with regard to technical, administrative and service-oriented issues relating to the utilization, transmission and maintenance of the PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content, as THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION may reasonably request.
perturbation.changed_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT may provide assistance to THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, with regard to certain technical, administrative and service-oriented issues if they find it pertinent, relating to the utilization, transmission and maintenance of the PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT 'will provide' assistance when 'reasonably requested'. This creates a clear obligation. The modified text changes this to 'may provide' and 'if they find it pertinent', making the provision of assistance discretionary and dependent on PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT's subjective assessment. This could contradict the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 1201(b)(20) defines good faith as 'honesty in fact and the observance of reasonable commercial standards of fair dealing.' By making assistance discretionary, PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT could potentially refuse to provide necessary assistance, even when reasonably required for the utilization of the content, thereby violating the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing inherent in contracts under California law. This is particularly concerning if the 'pertinent' criteria are not clearly defined and are used to unfairly withhold assistance.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 1201 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 1. GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1201. (a) Unless the context otherwise requires, words or phrases defined in this section, or in the additional definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof, have the meanings stated. (b) Subject to definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof: (1) “Action,” in the sense of a judicial proceeding, includes recoupment, counterclaim, setoff, suit in equity, and any other proceeding in which rights are determined. (2) “Aggrieved party” means a party entitled to pursue a remedy. (3) “Agreement,” as distinguished from “contract,” means the bargain of the parties in fact, as found in their language or inferred from other circumstances, including course of performance, course of dealing, or usage of trade as provided in Section 1303. (4) “Bank” means a person engaged in the business of banking, and includes a savings bank, savings and loan association, credit union, and trust company. (5) “Bearer” means a person in possessio
perturbation.justification: The modification from 'will provide' to 'may provide' fundamentally alters the obligation of PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT. The original text creates a clear duty to assist, while the changed text makes assistance discretionary. The LLM Law Explanation states that California Commercial Code § 1201(b)(20) defines good faith as 'honesty in fact and the observance of reasonable commercial standards of fair dealing.' By making assistance discretionary, PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT could refuse necessary assistance, even when reasonably required, potentially violating the implied duty of good faith. The provided snippet 1 defines key terms related to commercial code, setting the stage for the legal interpretation of 'good faith' and 'agreement.' The change introduces ambiguity around a core service obligation, which the original contract made mandatory, potentially undermining fair dealing. This fits the definition of an Ambiguities Legal Contradiction, as the ambiguity makes the contract difficult to enforce, potentially leading to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Within 30 days of the end of each quarter during the term of this agreement, PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT shall receive from THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, a detailed accounting statement showing Net Due and Paid Shared Revenues for that quarter and shall remit to PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT its share of such revenues.
perturbation.changed_text: Within a reasonable timeframe after the end of each quarter, THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION will attempt to provide PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT with an accounting statement regarding shared revenues, if any, and may remit a portion of such revenues as they deem appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a clear obligation for providing a detailed accounting statement and remitting shared revenues within 30 days. The modified text introduces ambiguity by replacing '30 days' with 'a reasonable timeframe', 'shall receive' with 'will attempt to provide', and 'Net Due and Paid Shared Revenues' with 'regarding shared revenues, if any', and replacing 'shall remit' with 'may remit a portion of such revenues as they deem appropriate.' This weakens the obligation significantly, allowing THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION discretion over the timing, content, and amount of payment.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1657 states that '[i]f no time is specified for the performance of an act required to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed. If the act is in its nature capable of being done instantly—as, for example, if it consists in the payment of money only—it must be performed immediately upon the thing to be done being exactly ascertained.' By replacing '30 days' with 'reasonable timeframe' and giving THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION discretion over remitting revenues, the modified clause violates § 1657 by failing to ensure prompt payment once the amount is ascertained. Furthermore, it allows them to avoid the obligation to remit any revenues, violating the spirit of the revenue sharing agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1657 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1657. If no time is specified for the performance of an act required to be performed, a reasonable time is allowed. If the act is in its nature capable of being done instantly—as, for example, if it consists in the payment of money only—it must be performed immediately upon the thing to be done being exactly ascertained. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts California Civil Code § 1657, as explained in the Law Explanation and supported by Scraped Law Snippet 1. The original text requires action 'within 30 days,' while the changed text uses the ambiguous term 'reasonable timeframe'. CIV 1657 states that if no time is specified, 'a reasonable time is allowed'. However, it also clarifies that if the act is the 'payment of money only—it must be performed immediately upon the thing to be done being exactly ascertained.' The changed text introduces ambiguity and discretion, allowing THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION to potentially delay or avoid payment, violating the requirement for prompt payment once the amount is ascertained. This creates a legal contradiction as the modified clause undermines the original intent and the requirements of CIV 1657.
